questions.question: Choose the daily routine phrasal verb: I {a1} early on weekdays.
Choose the study phrasal verb: Please {a1} the new word in a dictionary.
Choose the work phrasal verb: The team will {a1} the survey next week.
Choose the communication phrasal verb: Could you {a1} during the meeting?
Choose the travel phrasal verb: We {a1} the train at the last stop.
Choose the problem-solving phrasal verb: We need to {a1} this issue today.
Choose the relationship phrasal verb: She {a1} her colleagues very well.
Choose the phrasal verb from school context: Students must {a1} their essays by Friday.
Choose the phrasal verb that means arrange: They will {a1} a meeting with the client.
Choose the correct phrasal verb: We have to {a1} several complaints.
Choose the work phrasal verb: I will {a1} by email after the call.
Choose the communication phrasal verb: She {a1} the main risk in the plan.
